content_markdown: A black body has absolute temperature $T$ and surface area $A$. The intensity of the radiation emitted by the body is $I$. Another black body of surface area $2A$ has absolute temperature $2T$. What is the intensity of radiation emitted by this second black body?
vibe_explanation: According to the Stefan-Boltzmann law, the intensity $I$ (power per unit area) emitted by a black body is proportional to the fourth power of its absolute temperature ($I = \sigma T^4$). Note that intensity is defined as power per unit area, so it is independent of the surface area $A$. When the temperature doubles from $T$ to $2T$, the new intensity $I'$ becomes $\sigma (2T)^4 = 16 \sigma T^4 = 16I$.
